repeated_pain: Agents lose or mishandle project state across long-running work
workaround: Users create persistent memory files, custom instructions, hooks, and issue-search habits
evidence: Multiple issue threads discuss context, reliability, docs, usage, and workflow regressions
tool_or_process_replaced: Manual project notes, human code review, ad hoc session handoffs
monetization_hint: paid memory layer, workflow guardrails, team handoff tool
notes: Seed example. Replace with specific issue URLs and quotes during a scan.

repeated_pain: Agents lose context between sessions; users must re-explain project state; CLAUDE.md files exceed 250 lines causing compliance degradation
workaround: Local git-based systems with /handoff /sync /engineering commands; multiple markdown files (FEATURE_IMPL_PLAN.md + per-feature docs); global ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md at 15k tokens
evidence: wfn: maintains FEATURE_IMPL_PLAN.md + per-feature docs + CLAUDE.md under 250 lines; BonoboIO: 15k-token global ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md with conventions and custom slash commands; frumplestlatz: 'It just has to be local' (enterprise/privacy constraint)
tool_or_process_replaced: External memory SaaS; cloud-based agent memory tools
monetization_hint: Local-first memory management; git-backed session handoff tool
notes: Strong skepticism toward cloud solutions; users prefer local markdown in git; enterprise/privacy constraint is common objection to cloud memory

repeated_pain: Context silently disappears during long sessions (compression amnesia); memory locked to one tool and one machine; no provenance tracking or conflict resolution
workaround: CLAUDE.md and Rules files (acknowledged as insufficient); starting sessions fresh after tool switches
evidence: Bumblebiber: 'long conversations get compressed and context silently disappears — the agent forgets decisions made 2 hours ago'; 'Switch from Claude Code to Cursor, or from your laptop to your desktop, and everything is gone'; YaraDori raises need for provenance tracking + audit trails
tool_or_process_replaced: Per-session CLAUDE.md files; manual notes across tool switches
monetization_hint: Cross-tool portable memory; audit trail for agent decisions; conflict resolution for contradictory agent knowledge
notes: YaraDori articulates enterprise-grade requirements (provenance, audit trails) — signals buyers beyond solo devs

repeated_pain: Must restart from scratch each session; response quality degrades as conversation lengthens; agent forgets to use memory without repeated nudges
workaround: Builder implemented hooks to keep prompting Claude to use memory; zettelkasten-style notes stored in ChromaDB
evidence: Builder quote: 'I spend hours with claude digging into some functionality, and it was frustrating that I have to start from scratch next day/session'; hooks-as-reminder is described as required to prevent agent from ignoring memory
tool_or_process_replaced: Manual session summaries; re-explanation at session start
monetization_hint: Self-evolving knowledge graph; time-aware recall; hooks-based memory enforcement
notes: Builder's own pain drove tool creation (A-MEM); hooks needed to remind Claude to use its own memory — reveals that even with memory tools, agents don't reliably use them

repeated_pain: Sub-agents are stateless; domain expertise must be re-explained in every Task invocation; no accumulation of specialized knowledge across sessions
workaround: Manually prepending critical instructions to every agent prompt (e.g. 'CRITICAL: Use Unicode escape sequences for Java properties')
evidence: Issue: 'Currently ui-translator requires this in every prompt: CRITICAL: Use Unicode escape sequences (\uXXXX) for Java properties'; closed as duplicate referencing 6+ related issues (#4182 #1770 #3013 #87 #1813 #3081)
tool_or_process_replaced: Human expert hand-holding; repeated manual prompt engineering per task
monetization_hint: Agent-specific persistent memory files; cross-session specialization layer
notes: Closed as duplicate = high volume of similar reports; 123k repo stars indicates large affected population; 6+ duplicate issues is strong signal

repeated_pain: Agent re-explores files already analyzed in prior sessions; 10+ minute task overhead from context rebuilding each session
workaround: Built local proxy intercepting API calls; captures reasoning; stores in SQLite; auto-injects relevant context matched by file
evidence: 'I kept watching Claude Code spend 10 minutes re-exploring files it already analyzed yesterday'; task time reduced from 10-11 min to 1-2 min; ~250 npm downloads with no promotion; team synchronization dashboard built on top
tool_or_process_replaced: Manual session summaries; keeping sessions alive continuously to avoid context loss
monetization_hint: Automatic context capture + injection; team knowledge sync; token efficiency through intelligent filtering
notes: 250 npm downloads without promotion is strong organic demand signal; quantified time savings (10-11 min → 1-2 min) is credible and specific

repeated_pain: Native memory feature broken for months then silently removed; users lose all project context built up across sessions
workaround: Convert memories to project rules; create project.md files; use OpenSpec framework for spec-driven development; downgrade to v2.0.77
evidence: Maor Azoulay: 'memories persistence has been broken for me for months'; 'instead of fixing it they remove the feature altogether'; Dean Rie (support): 'known recurring issue affecting multiple users'
tool_or_process_replaced: Native Cursor memory feature; per-session re-explanation of project state
monetization_hint: Reliable cross-session memory that survives product updates; portable memory not tied to one tool version
notes: Feature removal drove users to manual workarounds — a durable-gap signal, not a temporary bug; structural unreliability erodes platform trust

repeated_pain: Session amnesia; hallucination from stale or missing context; hours of context re-establishment overhead per week
workaround: MEMORY.md as table-of-contents routing Claude to topic files; private-journal-mcp (engineering notebook + user-info notebook + search); claude-brain (1321 sessions, 67k+ messages, 9 projects); two-file injection: MEMORY.md ~130 lines + domain topic files
evidence: 'I built a memory OS after Claude Code hallucinated 42% of my code'; 'It Saves Me Hours Every Week'; claude-brain: running across 1321 sessions and 67000+ messages; cluster of 5+ 'I built X for Claude Code memory' posts on DEV Community and Medium
tool_or_process_replaced: Manual session notes; human context explanation at session start; repeated prompt engineering
monetization_hint: Managed memory service; session handoff automation; anti-hallucination memory layer
notes: Cluster of 5+ builder posts = reproducible pain driving DIY behavior; 42% hallucination rate quantification is extreme and credible pain signal; claude-brain scale (67k messages) shows users investing significant effort
